response.justification: Bogdan has a broad engineering background with some tangentially relevant experience — geospatial platforms (GISCollective, Green Map), distributed low-latency systems (Dunnhumby), and a brief C++ stint at National Instruments — but the role demands modern, commercial-grade C++ (or Rust) as a primary competency, which is clearly absent here. His current and recent work is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js, his C++ exposure is dated (2014–2015) and limited in scope, and while he has GIS experience, it centers on data collection and mapping rather than routing pipelines, tiling, or navigation systems.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map projects, including event-driven geo-data pipelines on Kubernetes
Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency design)
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across a long and varied career
concerns: No modern or commercial C++ experience; listed C++ use is a 2014–2015 IDE component role and a bachelor thesis project — neither reflects the depth required
No Rust experience whatsoever, eliminating the only acceptable alternative to C++
GIS experience is centered on data collection and visualization tooling, not routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or real-time traffic integration
